task_completion.description: Create the 'ssh_brute_force' eventtype with correct search and tags
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Event Types' settings page or creation dialog showing the name 'ssh_brute_force', a search string containing 'index=security_logs' and 'fail' (or 'Failed password'), and the tags 'authentication' and 'attack'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'ssh_successful_login' eventtype with correct search and tags
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Event Types' settings page or creation dialog showing the name 'ssh_successful_login', a search string containing 'index=security_logs' and 'accept' (or 'Accepted'), and the tags 'authentication' and 'privileged'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'system_error' eventtype with correct search and tags
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Event Types' settings page or creation dialog showing the name 'system_error', a search string containing 'index=system_logs' and 'error', and the tags 'operations' and 'error'.
Look for the Splunk 'Searches, Reports, and Alerts' settings page or a 'Save As Report' dialog showing a saved search explicitly named 'Tagged_Security_Summary'.

task_completion.description: Configure the saved search to query 'tag=authentication' and summarize by eventtype
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search bar or the saved search configuration details showing the query includes 'tag=authentication' and a summarizing command (e.g., '| stats count by eventtype', '| chart count by eventtype', or '| top eventtype').

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI or valid configuration files
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Splunk web interface (http://localhost:8000) or editing Splunk .conf files in a terminal, rather than just writing text in an unconnected editor.

integrity.description: Agent actually created the eventtypes and tags rather than mocking the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for progressive steps of filling out forms in Settings > Event Types and Settings > Tags, or running valid Splunk SPL commands to create them.

integrity.description: Agent executed the search before saving it
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Search & Reporting app showing the executed search 'tag=authentication ...' with actual results or a job running before it is saved as a report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Splunk credentials: admin / SplunkAdmin1!. Eventtypes to verify: 1) 'ssh_brute_force' (index='security_logs', keyword='fail' or 'Failed password', tags: 'authentication', 'attack'). 2) 'ssh_successful_login' (index='security_logs', keyword='accept' or 'Accepted', tags: 'authentication', 'privileged'). 3) 'system_error' (index='system_logs', keyword='error', tags: 'operations', 'error'). Saved search name: 'Tagged_Security_Summary' which must include 'tag=authentication' in its search string.
